🔬 Encypher Chrome Extension — Local Test Page

Use this page to verify the extension works against your localhost API

1. API Health Check

Checking...

Target: http://localhost:8000 (full-stack) or http://localhost:9000 (standalone)

2. Unsigned Content (No Embeddings)

This is regular text without any C2PA or Encypher signatures. The extension should not detect any embeddings in this paragraph. The Verify tab should show "No Signed Content" when viewing this page.

3. Signed Content Area

Paste signed text below (from the extension's Sign tab or API). The extension should detect the embedding and show a verification badge.

Paste signed content here...

4. Editor Detection Test

The extension should detect this textarea and show a floating "Sign" button when focused (if enabled in settings).

5. Setup Checklist

  • Start the API: docker compose -f docker-compose.full-stack.yml up (port 8000) or cd enterprise_api && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --port 9000
  • Load extension in Chrome: chrome://extensions → Developer mode → Load unpacked
  • Open extension Settings → Set API URL to http://localhost:8000 (full-stack) or http://localhost:9000 (standalone)
  • Add API key (or use demo_key for testing)
  • Open this page → Click extension icon → Verify the Debug tab appears (🐛)
  • Use the Sign tab to sign text → Paste into area #3 above
  • Verify badge appears on signed content

6. Page Console Log

Extension-related console messages from this page:

Waiting for events...
